context: ◊  15-100 times more common than primary skeletal neoplasms! SOLITARY BONE LESION ◊  Of all causes only 7% are due to metastasis ◊  In patients with known malignancy solitary bone lesions are due to metastasis (55%), due to trauma (25%), due to infection (10%) Location:   axial skeleton (64-68%), ribs (45%), extremities (24%), skull (12%) mnemonic:   Several Kinds Of Horribly Nasty Tumors Leap Promptly To Bone Breast cancer:        extensive osteolytic lesions; involvement of entire skeleton; pathologic fractures common Thyroid / kidney:    often solitary; rapid progression with bone expansion
